---
title: "Canada Observatory"
subtitle: "Data and charts for informed civic conversations"
---

![](visual_assets/brand/botanical/leaf-botanical-cells.svg){.hero-leaf fig-alt="The Canada Observatory mark: a sugar-maple leaf divided into six colour cells"}

Canada Observatory exists to support informed conversations about Canada with
relevant, timely and reliable data. It provides interactive, regularly updated
charts and downloadable data on the state of the country, organized around six themes —
**people & society**, **economy & affordability**, **government & public
finances**, **health & well-being**, **land & environment**, and **education,
science & innovation**. All data comes from authoritative primary sources —
primarily [Statistics Canada](https://www.statcan.gc.ca/), the
[OECD](https://data.oecd.org/), and other government and international agencies.
Most of it refreshes automatically — the pipeline checks its sources daily — with
the rest updated on each source's own release schedule.

Where possible, Canada is shown alongside a broad set of 17 OECD peer countries
(not just the G7) to provide meaningful international context.

Where the data allows, maps reach all the way down to the neighbourhood level.

Data files are freely downloadable in easy-to-use formats — for students,
journalists, researchers, and anyone who wants accessible Canadian data.

---

## Start with these questions

We want to help you explore and better understand your own questions about Canada.
To get started, here are a few of the big topics on people's minds right now — each
links to the section behind it:

- [**Is Canada becoming less affordable?**](housing/) — inflation, house prices, rents, and household debt
- [**Are incomes keeping up?**](income/) — wages, real incomes, and poverty
- [**How do Canadian living standards compare with peer countries?**](economics/) — GDP per capita, productivity, and jobs
- [**Where is population growth coming from?**](population/) — immigration, temporary residents, and natural increase
- [**How big is government, and what does it spend on?**](fiscal/) — debt, deficits, the public-service workforce, and federal spending
- [**Is crime going up?**](wellbeing/crime-safety.qmd) — everyday crime, hate crime, and your city, over time
- [**How fast is Canada warming?**](environment/climate-change.qmd) — national, regional, and city-level temperature trends
- [**How long do Canadians live?**](health/) — a century of life expectancy, the peer comparison, and the recent dip
- [**How many children are Canadians having?**](population/index.qmd#fertility) — fertility over the long run, here and among peers
- [**What does my neighbourhood look like?**](population/diversity.qmd) — diversity, religion, incomes, and home values, mapped to the census tract

---

## Topics

::::{.columns}

:::{.column .area-card .area-people width="32%"}
![](visual_assets/brand/motifs/banner-people.svg){.area-banner}

### [People & Society](population/overview.qmd)
How many people live in Canada and why that's changing; diversity, religion,
and crime & safety — with maps down to the neighbourhood level.

*Sources: Statistics Canada, Toronto Police Service*
:::

:::{.column width="2%"}
:::

:::{.column .area-card .area-economy width="32%"}
![](visual_assets/brand/motifs/banner-economy.svg){.area-banner}

### [Economy & Affordability](economics/overview.qmd)
Growth, jobs, the cost of living, housing, and incomes & inequality — Canada
against 17 OECD peers.

*Sources: OECD, Statistics Canada, Bank of Canada, CREA*
:::

:::{.column width="2%"}
:::

:::{.column .area-card .area-government width="32%"}
![](visual_assets/brand/motifs/banner-government.svg){.area-banner}

### [Government & Public Finances](fiscal/overview.qmd)
Debt, deficits, and interest costs compared with peers; the public-sector
workforce; and what the federal government spends, back to 1961.

*Sources: Statistics Canada, OECD, Treasury Board*
:::

::::

::::{.columns}

:::{.column .area-card .area-health width="32%"}
![](visual_assets/brand/motifs/banner-health.svg){.area-banner}

### [Health & Well-being](health/overview.qmd)
Life expectancy, health spending and system capacity, population health and
risk factors, substance use, and life satisfaction.

*Sources: OECD, World Bank/WHO, PHAC, CIHI, World Happiness Report*
:::

:::{.column width="2%"}
:::

:::{.column .area-card .area-environment width="32%"}
![](visual_assets/brand/motifs/banner-environment.svg){.area-banner}

### [Land & Environment](geography/overview.qmd)
The land itself — ecozones, elevation, water, agriculture, wildfire — plus
climate change, air quality, emissions, and energy.

*Sources: ECCC, NRCan, Statistics Canada, OECD, Our World in Data*
:::

:::{.column width="2%"}
:::

:::{.column .area-card .area-education width="32%"}
![](visual_assets/brand/motifs/banner-education.svg){.area-banner}

### [Education, Science & Innovation](education/overview.qmd)
University tuition across provinces and fields, R&D spending, and Canada's
research workforce, compared with peers.

*Sources: Statistics Canada, OECD*
:::

::::

---

## Principles

- **Authoritative sources only** — Statistics Canada, OECD, and other government, international, and research agencies
- **Broad peer comparisons** — 17 OECD countries, not just the G7
- **Automatically updated** — the data pipeline checks its sources daily; update frequency varies by source
- **Open and downloadable** — all cleaned data available as CSV
- **No policy advocacy** — we present the data, sources, and methods to support
  informed discussion, not to push conclusions. We make
  [one deliberate exception](about/open-data.qmd): we advocate for the creation,
  use, and open availability of public data itself — without it, nothing on
  this site exists.

---

Follow along on [Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/canadaobservatory/) and
[X](https://x.com/canobservatory).
